concerns.text: Tasks T028–T032 (Scaling Law & Cost Analysis) have no corresponding Functional Requirement (FR) or Success Criterion (SC) in spec.md. The spec defines FR-001 through FR-008 and SC-001 through SC-005, none of which mandate scaling law exponents, cost-metabolism calculations, or singularity detection. This represents unapproved scope expansion.

Task T028 implements a 'scaling law analyzer' fitting power-law models to code coverage. No FR or SC in spec.md requires this analysis. The spec focuses on correlation (FR-005), contamination (FR-006), and sensitivity (FR-007), but does not mention scaling exponents or model parameter correlation.

Task T029 implements a 'cost-metabolism calculator' for 'bureaucratic overhead'. This concept and metric are absent from spec.md FRs/SCs. The spec does not define 'cost of bug-free code' or 'bureaucratic overhead' as required outputs.

Task T030 extends the schema to include 'scaling exponents' and 'singularity detection flags'. Since the underlying FRs/SCs do not require these metrics, the schema extension is unsupported by the specification.

Task T031 updates visualization scripts for 'scaling law plots' and 'metabolism curves'. These visualizations correspond to the unapproved Scaling Law phase (Phase 6) and have no basis in spec.md FR-008 (which only mandates radar charts, heatmaps, and correlation plots).
Task T032 provides unit tests for 'scaling law fitting logic'. Testing logic for a feature (Scaling Laws) that is not defined in the spec constitutes scope creep and lacks a requirement anchor.

Tasks T028–T032 are incorrectly tagged with [US3]. User Story 3 (Sensitivity & Contamination) in spec.md covers temperature variance and contamination filtering. It does not cover scaling laws or cost analysis. These tasks should either be untagged (if the feature is removed) or the spec must be amended to include a new User Story for Scaling Laws.
